message:
feat(caut): Cx-first structured-concurrency entry points (ft-xbnl0.2.2)

Add Cx-threaded variants for CautClient's public async API so subprocess
timeouts bind to the caller's capability context instead of reaching
into the ambient runtime. Promotes runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx from
pub(crate) to pub.

New Cx-first API (asupersync-runtime only):
- CautClient::usage_cx(&self, cx: &Cx, service)
- CautClient::refresh_cx(&self, cx: &Cx, service)

Implementation:
- Split the old run() into run_with_cx (Cx-first, uses timeout_with_cx)
  and run_inner (non-asupersync fallback, uses ambient timeout).
- Shared finalize_output helper handles exit status + size bounds.
- Existing usage/refresh now build a request Cx via cx::for_request()
  under asupersync-runtime and delegate to the _cx variant (matching
  the pool.rs::acquire_with_cx and retry.rs::with_retry_cx patterns).

New deterministic LabRuntime test:
- caut_client_usage_cx_runs_under_labruntime (seed 0x4C61_6231_C410_C0C0):
  drives usage_cx against a nonexistent binary under virtual time and
  asserts NotInstalled in <2s wall-clock. If a wall-clock timeout or
  ambient runtime handle sneaks back in, the test step-explodes or
  burns real seconds.

This is the second file in the ft-xbnl0.2.2 Cx-first lane (after
retry.rs / f4fbb7d4). Remaining inventory: 13 files, largest of which
(runtime.rs) is still owned by wa-1m7nk.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc1-target):
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features --features native-wezterm caut::: 65/65 pass
- cargo clippy --lib --no-default-features --features native-wezterm -- -D warnings: clean
- cargo check --lib (default features): pass
- scripts/check_no_runtime_regression.sh: ft-xbnl0.2.6 gate still passes
- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check: clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
